_meta.note: PUBLIC DEMO FIXTURE. Fictional data only. RM-tier surface. No numeric RIPI score exists in this file by design. No divergence/manager/coaching data. Cast matches the marketing mockups so a prospect who sees a screenshot and connects the MCP lands on the same people.

contacts.name: Isabella Chen
contacts.title: SVP Partnerships
contacts.briefMe: Isabella is detail-oriented and data-driven — she'll want specifics on the Q3 metrics before committing to anything. She flagged last quarter that response times felt slow; come in ready to address that directly. Her team is mid-reorg, so anything affecting timeline is a sensitivity.
contacts.cadenceNote: Cadence has slipped to Red this month. She reached out twice before your last reply — the next response sets the tone. Be specific, be fast.

contacts.name: Priya Nair
contacts.title: Director, Strategic Alliances
contacts.briefMe: New to this account — first meeting. She's been briefed by Isabella and is likely evaluating fit. Warm, collaborative tone. Ask her what success looks like from her seat.

contacts.name: Taylor Simmons
contacts.title: VP Finance
contacts.briefMe: Finance just signed off on the 6-month pilot — momentum is on your side. Taylor moves on evidence and clear next steps. Confirm scope and the success metric in writing while goodwill is high.

contacts.name: Nadia Kim
contacts.title: Head of EMEA Expansion
contacts.briefMe: Recently introduced via Amir — the relationship is still forming. She leads EMEA expansion, so anything you bring should connect to a London/EMEA angle. Early days: invest in a real first conversation before pitching.

contacts.name: Amir Patel
contacts.title: Managing Partner
contacts.briefMe: A strong, generous connector — he just made a warm intro to Nadia. Relationship is healthy across the board. Best move here is to close the loop: thank him, and tell him what came of the intro. Reciprocity keeps this one warm.

contacts.name: Raj Johal
contacts.title: Director of Operations
contacts.briefMe: Just spent real time in the product and came back with specific first impressions — that's an engaged evaluator, not a tire-kicker. Follow up on the things that stood out to him; he's telling you where the value landed.

contacts.name: Brianna Wells
contacts.title: Head of Procurement
contacts.briefMe: She flagged early that the annual vendor review starts soon — that's a buying-window signal, not small talk. Cadence has gone quiet at exactly the wrong time. Re-engage now with something concrete tied to the review timeline, before the process locks.
contacts.cadenceNote: No touchpoint in 34 days, heading into a vendor review window. This is the one to re-open this week.
